name: Rejection of Goods
text: The Buyer shall have the right to inspect all Goods upon delivery. The Buyer may reject any Goods that do not conform to the agreed specifications, quality standards, or warranty requirements by issuing a written rejection notice to the Supplier within fifteen (15) days of delivery (the 'Rejection Period'). The rejection notice shall specify in reasonable detail the grounds for rejection. Upon receipt of a valid rejection notice, the Supplier shall, at its own cost and expense, collect the rejected Goods and, at the Buyer's election, either: (a) replace the rejected Goods with conforming Goods within seven (7) days; or (b) refund the purchase price paid for the rejected Goods. Failure to issue a rejection notice within the Rejection Period shall be deemed acceptance of the Goods, without prejudice to any latent defect claims under the Sale of Goods Act, 1930.
invalid_if: Rejection period less than 7 days for goods requiring technical inspection
Rejection rights purportedly waived at time of order
title: Rejection of Goods
